neighborhoods.name: Downtown / Ohio City
neighborhoods.description: Revitalized downtown with Rock Hall, West Side Market, and a growing craft brewery scene
neighborhoods.character: Revitalized, Rock Hall, West Side Market

housing.predominantType: Warehouse lofts, condos, and renovated buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Cleveland. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Lakewood
neighborhoods.description: Dense inner-ring suburb with walkable downtown, independent shops, and Lake Erie access
neighborhoods.character: Walkable inner suburb, independent shops, lake access

housing.predominantType: Colonial homes, doubles, and older apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Cleveland's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Parma
neighborhoods.description: Southern suburb with strong Eastern European heritage, affordable housing, and community events

housing.predominantType: Cape Cods, ranch houses, and bungalows

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Shaker Heights
neighborhoods.description: Historic garden suburb with excellent schools, Shaker Square, and rapid transit to downtown
neighborhoods.character: Garden suburb, excellent schools, rapid transit

housing.predominantType: Tudor homes, Colonials, and estate properties

neighborhoods.character_notes: Family-friendly area with good schools, parks, and community amenities. Popular with active retirees and families.
